rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks only into implementation scopes when binding implementation-level data flows, preserving AADL component structure.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: use component type annexes for visible I/O contracts, implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Only reference ports or variables that are explicitly declared and visible in the target scope.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes in implementation scope must use assign for bindings; avoid guarantees in implementation scope.
Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in component type annexes without intermediate variables or outputs.
Do not encode event ports as Boolean or numeric values unless a model-provided data value exists in scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
